response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior full-stack/web engineer with strong software craftsmanship credentials, but the role requires deep hardware-adjacent expertise — SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. Brief exposure to C/C++ tooling at National Instruments and low-latency DLang services is the closest match, but it falls far short of principal-level performance architecture for deep-learning workloads.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, exceeding the years-of-experience bar
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (DLang, async I/O, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Some C/C++ and embedded/hardware-adjacent experience (NI LabWindows/CVI, robotic arms)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, training, or inference optimization background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not silicon or systems performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering seniority and low-latency systems exposure
